title: [InstCombine] Fold `(icmp eq/ne (add nuw x, y), 0)` -> `(icmp eq/ne (or x, y), 0)`
summary:
The developers generalized an optimization by recognizing a core pattern: `(add nuw A, B) == 0` can be transformed into `(or A, B) == 0`. This works because `nuw` (no unsigned wrap) ensures the addition behaves as a bitwise OR for the zero-equality check—both operations yield zero only when all input bits are zero.

They started from a specific case involving `zext` and widened it to handle any `nuw` addition, regardless of operand types or extensions. The patch inserts a targeted fold in `foldICmpBinOpEqualityWithConstant`, checking for `nuw` and creating an `or` comparison instead. This captures the original test and new variants like `i8` or plain `nuw` adds.

Crucially, they added negative tests (`multiuse`, `dec_notnonzero`) to ensure the transform only applies when safe and profitable—avoiding invalid rewrites or missed optimizations. The approach demonstrates how to lift a narrow pattern into a robust, type-agnostic rule by leveraging existing IR properties (`nuw`) and validating with diverse test cases.
